title: Housing Complaints * Public Housing Residents - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Legal & Public Safety > Housing Complaints * Public Housing Residents 16 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter Mobile Home Residents Public Housing Residents Section 8 Housing Residents Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF HOME FORWARD PROPERTY MANAGEMENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Maintains public housing units for low-income residents in which residents pay an affordable percentage of their income as rent. 135 SW Ash Street Portland, OR 97204 Eligibility: Eligibility varies by community. However, generally to be eligible: -Household income must not exceed the area median income percentage for the household size -At least one person in the household must be a citizen or permanent resident of the United States -Household size must meet occupancy guidelines. One bedroom is allowed for the head of the household and co-head/spouse, and one bedroom is allowed for every two people after that, regardless of age or gender -Applicants can apply for all waitlists they qualify for, regardless of whether they previously applied during other openings Priority status granted to: -Individuals who have a terminal illness and are diagnosed by a medical provider with a life expectancy of 12 months or less -Individuals with a disability who require housing with access features such as wheelchair accommodations and individuals eligible for in-home Medicaid services that require assistance with meals, bathing, laundry, and housekeeping to stop or delay premature entry into a nursing facility or adult foster care Hours: Office Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm, by appointment Email (503) 802-8300 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HOMES FOR GOOD PUBLIC HOUSING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides information about federally-subsidized private housing. Residents pay no more than 30% of their adjusted income for rent and utilities. 100 W 13th Avenue Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Low-income families (two or more individuals), single people age 62 and older, or adults with disabilities. Income limits can be found on the website. Applicants are subject to a background check and other tenant screening criteria. Individuals with a registered sex offense will be denied. Criminal activities are reviewed on a case-by-case basis. Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-4pm Email (541) 682-3755 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HOUSING AUTHORITY OF CLACKAMAS COUNTY HOUSING AUTHORITY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides information, referrals, and support for landlord-tenant issues, fair housing, and complaints of housing discrimination. Maintains listings of low-income housing in Clackamas County, including non-subsidized low-cost apartments. Manages Housing Authority-owned units, subsidized rental housing, and the Section 8/Housing Choice Voucher program. Handles complaints related to public housing and Section 8. 13930 S Gain Street Oregon City, OR 97045 Eligibility: Varies by program or property Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-5pm, holiday hours posted on website Email (503) 655-8267 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HOUSING AUTHORITY OF LINCOLN COUNTY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides information about affordable housing. Administers Section 8/Housing Choice Vouchers and public housing, in addition to fielding complaints about those programs. 1039 NW Nye Street Newport, OR 97365 Eligibility: Varies by program. Hours: Monday-Thursday 8:30am-noon and 1pm-4pm Email (541) 265-5326 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HOUSING AUTHORITY OF MALHEUR AND HARNEY COUNTIES HOUSING AUTHORITY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Administers the following housing programs: -Section 8 Housing Choice Voucher Program: As of April 2018, Section 8 is accepting pre-applications for the wait list and has a 12-month wait time -Family Self-Sufficiency Program: Helps Section 8 recipients obtain education and job skills in order to become financially self-sufficient -Housing Choice Voucher Homeownership Program: Helps Section 8 recipients receive assistance toward a monthly mortgage payment -Low-Rent Public Housing Program: Provides federally subsidized rental housing -Farm Labor Housing: Offers housing for active and retired agricultural workers Also fields complaints about Section 8 and public housing programs. 959 Fortner Street Ontario, OR 97914 Eligibility: -Low-income households; applicants from outside Malheur and Harney Counties must reside in Malheur or Harney County for one year before requesting to move to another county. -Farm labor housing is available to domestic agricultural workers including those working on fish and oyster farms and on-farm processing; can be retired and/or disabled from agricultural work; must be a U.S. citizen or permanent resident. Available to laborers that handle any raw materials. Hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-noon and 1pm-5pm Email (541) 889-9661 101 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HOUSING AUTHORITY OF YAMHILL COUNTY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides Housing Choice Voucher (HCV) rental assistance, including a Homeownership Voucher Program. Also offers a Family Self-Sufficiency program, Housing Rehab program, and manages several area affordable housing complexes for families, farmworkers, older adults, and people with special needs. 135 NE Dunn Place Mcminnville, OR 97128 Eligibility: Varies; some programs may have residency or income requirements. Call for information. Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-noon and 1pm-5pm Email (503) 883-4300 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LINN BENTON HOUSING AUTHORITY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides housing for low-income residents and undertakes activities necessary to accomplish this public purpose, including acquiring and managing properties, developing housing, issuing tax-exempt bonds, entering into mortgages and partnerships, borrowing money, and accepting grants. Residents of properties managed by the Linn-Benton Housing Authority may contact the Authority to lodge housing complaints. 1250 SE Queen Avenue Albany, OR 97322 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-noon and 1pm-5pm Email (541) 926-4497 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MARION COUNTY HOUSING AUTHORITY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Marion County Housing Authority (MCHA), under contract with the US Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD), administers the Section 8 Voucher Program and several other rental assistance programs. MCHA also owns and manages several affordable apartment complexes for seniors, families, and farm laborers. Serves the rural areas of Marion County, which are outside the Urban Growth Boundary of the City of Salem. No assistance is available for Salem/Keizer residents. 2645 Portland Road NE Suite 200 Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Eligibility varies by program. HUD-established income limits apply. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-noon and 1pm-4:30pm, closed federal holidays Email (503) 798-4170 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MID-COLUMBIA AND COLUMBIA GORGE HOUSING AUTHORITIES HOUSING CHOICE VOUCHER SECTION 8 Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides affordable, tenant-based housing assistance for individuals who face barriers due to income, disability, or age. Assistance follows the tenant as long as they remain eligible. 500 E 2nd Street The Dalles, OR 97058 Eligibility: Low-income individuals and families Hours: Monday-Thursday 8:30am-noon and 1pm-4pm and by appointment Email (541) 296-5462 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NORTH BEND CITY COOS-CURRY HOUSING AUTHORITIES HOUSING AUTHORITY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides residents of the community with information about and access to affordable housing. Section 8 Housing Choice Voucher Program is tenant based assistance, meaning assistance follows the tenant as long as they remain eligible for the program. The Housing Authority is not the landlord but assists with rent payments to the landlords. Public Housing units are owned and managed by the Housing Authority. This program provides unit-based assistance, meaning if a family moves out of the unit, the assistance remains with the unit. One small pet is allowed with prior approval before moving into a unit. 1700 Monroe Street North Bend, OR 97459 Eligibility: Must meet income guidelines Hours: Monday-Thursday 8:30am-noon and 12:45pm-4:30pm Email (541) 756-4111 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NORTHEAST OREGON HOUSING AUTHORITY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers low-income and subsidized apartments. Available programs: Section 8 Housing Choice Vouchers, Veterans Supportive Housing Vouchers, Section 8 Housing Voucher Homeownership, Mental Health Referred Housing, Family Self-Sufficiency, HUD Multi-Family Housing, Tax Credit Housing, and Rural Development Housing. 2608 May Lane La Grande, OR 97850 Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Monday-Thursday 8:30am-noon and 1pm-4:30pm Email (541) 963-5360 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NORTHWEST OREGON HOUSING AUTHORITY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Administers federal housing programs. Maintains a list of affordable housing and a bulletin board of local properties, including subsidized properties that are independently managed. Fields complaints about public housing and Section 8 housing. 147 S Main Avenue Warrenton, OR 97146 Eligibility: Varies by program Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-noon and 1pm-4pm; by appointment only Email (503) 861-0119 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SALEM HOUSING AUTHORITY PUBLIC HOUSING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Manages multiple income-based rental public housing properties and fields complaints about public housing. 360 Church Street SE Salem, OR 973013707 Eligibility: Low-income families, older adults, and people with disabilities with US citizenship or eligible immigration status Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-noon and 1pm-4pm. Closed all major holidays. Email (503) 588-6368 Get Directions Visit Website More Details UMATILLA COUNTY HOUSING AUTHORITY HOUSING AUTHORITY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Administers the federal and public housing programs including the Section 8 Housing Voucher program and manages several low-income apartment complexes. Listings of low-income properties, rural areas, and properties for agricultural workers. 155 SW Tenth Street Hermiston, OR 97838 Eligibility: -Low-income households -Section 8: Income limits are 30% of median household income for the county (chart on website under public housing) -Programs prioritize families with children, older adults, and people with disabilities Hours: Monday/Tuesday/Thursday/Friday 9am-12pm and 1pm-4pm, Wednesday 1pm-4pm Email (541) 567-3241 Get Directions Visit Website More Details UNITED STATES DEPARTMENT OF HOUSING AND URBAN DEVELOPMENT FAIR HOUSING LAWS AND COMPLAINTS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides information about fair housing laws. The Office of Fair Housing and Equal Opportunity (FHEO) records consumer complaints of unfair treatment or housing discrimination based on race, color, national origin, religion, sex, disability, familial status (i.e., presence of children in the household), sexual orientation, gender identity, and/or marital status. Public and Indian Housing (PIH) Resource Center: Handles questions or complaints about HUD rental programs, including Housing Choice (Section 8) Vouchers. Multifamily Housing Complaint Line: Enables residents of HUD-insured and -assisted properties and other community members to report complaints with a property's management concerning matters such as poor maintenance, dangers to health and safety, mismanagement, and fraud. HUD Inspector General Hotline: Takes reports of fraud, waste, abuse, or gross mismanagement in HUD or HUD-funded programs from HUD employees, contractors, and the public. Manufactured Housing Complaints: Handles manufactured/mobile home complaints unresolved by reporting to the business who sold the home and the manufacturer that produced the home. Title 1 and 203(k) Complaints: Report deceptive contractors who renovated and repaired homes through the Title 1 or 203(k) programs. 451 7th Street SW Washington, DC 20410 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Not applicable (800) 685-8470 - Get Directions Visit Website More Details WEST VALLEY HOUSING AUTHORITY PUBLIC HOUSING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Manages multiple income-based rental public housing properties and fields complaints about public housing. 204 SW Walnut Avenue Dallas, OR 97338 Eligibility: Income must be at or below 80% of the Area Median Income Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-5pm Email (503) 623-8387 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HOME FORWARD PROPERTY MANAGEMENT HOMES FOR GOOD PUBLIC HOUSING HOUSING AUTHORITY OF CLACKAMAS COUNTY HOUSING AUTHORITY HOUSING AUTHORITY OF LINCOLN COUNTY HOUSING AUTHORITY OF LINCOLN COUNTY HOUSING AUTHORITY OF MALHEUR AND HARNEY COUNTIES HOUSING AUTHORITY HOUSING AUTHORITY OF YAMHILL COUNTY HOUSING AUTHORITY OF YAMHILL COUNTY LINN BENTON HOUSING AUTHORITY LINN BENTON HOUSING AUTHORITY MARION COUNTY HOUSING AUTHORITY MARION COUNTY HOUSING AUTHORITY MID-COLUMBIA AND COLUMBIA GORGE HOUSING AUTHORITIES HOUSING CHOICE VOUCHER SECTION 8 NORTH BEND CITY COOS-CURRY HOUSING AUTHORITIES HOUSING AUTHORITY NORTHEAST OREGON HOUSING AUTHORITY NORTHEAST OREGON HOUSING AUTHORITY NORTHWEST OREGON HOUSING AUTHORITY NORTHWEST OREGON HOUSING AUTHORITY SALEM HOUSING AUTHORITY PUBLIC HOUSING UMATILLA COUNTY HOUSING AUTHORITY HOUSING AUTHORITY WEST VALLEY HOUSING AUTHORITY PUBLIC HOUSING
